# Case: Sandlands Vineyards Case*Created: Monday, October 11, 2021 at 3:36:09?PM*<div><b><span style="font-size: 22px">Case:</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span></b><b><span style="font-size: 22px">Sandlands Vineyards Case</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></b></div>
<div><b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></b></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px">Conduct a detailed industry analysis. The level 3 analysis should be included in the text of the paper; the level 1 and level 2 analyses should be included in the paperŐs exhibits. </span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px">Given the page limit for exhibits, the total number of exhibits for the level 1 analysis will count as 1 page of exhibits (e.g. if you have 5 pages of level 1 exhibits, the total exhibit count = 1 page of exhibits).</span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<ol>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 22px">How attractive is the industry?</span></b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<ul>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">S</span><span style="font-size: 22px">trong</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> in the </span><span style="font-size: 22px">USA</span><span style="font-size: 22px">. </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Total </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Sales</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">within</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">U</span><span style="font-size: 22px">.S. </span><span style="font-size: 22px">in 2016</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">were</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">399 mill</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ion</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">cases wi</span><span style="font-size: 22px">th a retail</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">value of </span><span style="font-size: 22px">$6</span><span style="font-size: 22px">0 </span><span style="font-size: 22px">billi</span><span style="font-size: 22px">on. </span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
</ul>
</ol>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<ol>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 22px">What is SandlandsŐ business level strategy? </span></b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<ul>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">Te</span><span style="font-size: 22px">gan </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Pass</span><span style="font-size: 22px">alc</span><span style="font-size: 22px">qua</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">fo</span><span style="font-size: 22px">und </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Ň</span><span style="font-size: 22px">old vine</span><span style="font-size: 22px">Ó </span><span style="font-size: 22px">vin</span><span style="font-size: 22px">eyard</span><span style="font-size: 22px">,</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">old vi</span><span style="font-size: 22px">nes </span><span style="font-size: 22px">had deep</span><span style="font-size: 22px">er roots </span><span style="font-size: 22px">up t</span><span style="font-size: 22px">o 4</span><span style="font-size: 22px">0 </span><span style="font-size: 22px">feet d</span><span style="font-size: 22px">eeper</span><span style="font-size: 22px">. </span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">U</span><span style="font-size: 22px">s</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ing</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Tu</span><span style="font-size: 22px">rl</span><span style="font-size: 22px">eyŐs</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> fa</span><span style="font-size: 22px">cilities</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> and </span><span style="font-size: 22px">equip</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ment. </span></li>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">75</span><span style="font-size: 22px">%</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> o</span><span style="font-size: 22px">f the wines</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> to customer</span><span style="font-size: 22px">s directly</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">and 2</span><span style="font-size: 22px">5%</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> throu</span><span style="font-size: 22px">gh two </span><span style="font-size: 22px">dis</span><span style="font-size: 22px">tribut</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ors loc</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ated in </span><span style="font-size: 22px">the la</span><span style="font-size: 22px">rgest</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">markets in Cal</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ifornia</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> and N</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ew York</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> and </span><span style="font-size: 22px">third in </span><span style="font-size: 22px">C</span><span style="font-size: 22px">olor</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ado</span><span style="font-size: 22px">. </span></li>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">Start</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ed by </span><span style="font-size: 22px">allocating</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> to peopl</span><span style="font-size: 22px">e who </span><span style="font-size: 22px">bought </span><span style="font-size: 22px">every year</span><span style="font-size: 22px">, then </span><span style="font-size: 22px">to</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> the peopl</span><span style="font-size: 22px">e that</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> have </span><span style="font-size: 22px">bought t</span><span style="font-size: 22px">he most</span><span style="font-size: 22px">, </span><span style="font-size: 22px">finally</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">look</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> to the peop</span><span style="font-size: 22px">le w</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ho are buying</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> for the first</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> time. </span></li>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">So</span><span style="font-size: 22px">cia</span><span style="font-size: 22px">l </span><span style="font-size: 22px">Media</span><span style="font-size: 22px">, w</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ebsite, </span><span style="font-size: 22px">new </span><span style="font-size: 22px">technolog</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ies</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">and</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">apps</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> such</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> as Vine</span><span style="font-size: 22px">Spr</span><span style="font-size: 22px">ing</span><span style="font-size: 22px">. </span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
</ul>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 22px">What activities underlie this strategy and how do they contribute to Value and Cost? </span></b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<ol>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">Does SandlandsŐ have an advantage --include a Value minus Cost analysis (V-C)</span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<ol>
<li><span style="font-size: 20px">Yes, Sandlands vineyards haveĘ</span><b><span style="font-size: 20px">a sustainable competitive advantage</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px">Ęin the premium wine market. Approximately 70% of the wine companies in the US break even or lose money due to sales. Greater percentage lose</span><span style="font-size: 20px"> </span><span style="font-size: 20px">money on an economic basis for example, after a charge for the cost of equity.</span><span style="font-size: 29px"> </span><font color="#E4AF0A"><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></font></li>
</ol>
</ol>
<li><b><font color="#E4AF0A"><span style="font-size: 22px">Is</span></font></b><b><span style="font-size: 22px"> SandlandsŐ position sustainable? </span></b><b><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></b></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Opportunities</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> are macro environment factors and developments that Sandlands Passalacqua can leverage either to consolidate existing market position or use them for further expansion. Opportunities can emerge from various factors such as - technological innovations, economic growth, political developments &amp policy changes, changes in consumer preferences, and increase in consumer disposable income</span><span style="font-size: 20px">.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Threats</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> are macro environment factors and developments that can derail business model of Sandlands Passalacqua. Threats can emerge from various factors such as - technological innovations, political developments &amp policy changes, increase in consumer disposable income, changes in consumer preferences, and economic growth . - Squeezing Middle Class in Developed and Developing World Đ The growing inequality is one of the biggest threat to not only globalization but also to capitalism.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Weaknesses</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> are the areas, capabilities or skills in which Sandlands Passalacqua lacks. It limits the ability of the firm to build a sustainable competitive advantage. Weaknesses come from lack or absence of five key resources &amp capabilities - physical resources such as land, building, financial resources, human resources, past experiences and successes, and activities &amp processes</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Strengths</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> are the</span><b><span style="font-size: 20px">ĘSandlands</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px">ĘPassalacqua capabilities and resources that it can leverage to build a</span><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Ęsustainable</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px">Ęcompetitive advantage in the marketplace. Strengths come from positive aspects of five key resources &amp capabilities - financial resources, past experiences and successes, human resources, activities &amp processes, and physical resources such as land, building . - High Margins Đ</span><b><span style="font-size: 20px">ĘSandlands Passalacqua</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px">Ęcharges a premium compare to its competitors.</span><br></li>
</ol>
<div><img style="max-width: 100%; max-height: 100%;" src="data:image/png;base64,"/><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<ol>
<ol>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">Apply the VRIO framework. </span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
<ol>
<li><span style="font-size: 19px">The VRIO framework isĘ</span><b><span style="font-size: 19px">a strategy tool that helps organisations identify the resources and capabilities that give them a sustained competitive advantage</span></b><span style="font-size: 19px">. ... The VRIO analysis is an internal analysis that helps determine the quality and usefulness of a firm's resources and capabilities. VRIO is an acronym for: Valuable.</span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
</ol>
</ol>
</ol>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<ol>
<li><span style="font-size: 22px">Based on your analysis, what should Sandlands do next? Consider the pros/cons of your recommendations; also consider each recommendationŐs impact on the industry conditions</span><span style="font-size: 22px"> </span><span style="font-size: 22px">and on SandlandsŐ V-C position.</span><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></li>
</ol>
<div><span style="font-size: 22px"><br></span></div>
<ul>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Sandlands Vineyards</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> does have a marketing strategy although not well defined and articulated. As stated by the owner Passalacqua their marketing objective is to make affordable wines that their friends could drink. Their marketing strategy could be summed up by four 5Ps of the marketing mix:</span></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Product:</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> Make unique, good quality, forgotten classic California variety wines using grapes from old vineyards with no intervention in the winemaking process and without using pesticides (organic) which people could enjoy.</span></li>
<li><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Price:</span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"> Affordable yet premium wines with a target retail price of $24 to $28 range per bottle</span><br></li>
</ul>
<div><br></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 21px"><h2>Strategic implementation: sand lands farm case study implementation</h2></span><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 21px"><h2><br></h2></span></font></div>
<ul>
<li><span style="font-size: 18px">In the case of Sandlands Vineyard, one of the possible implications for strategy is developing an online promotional campaign to reach the most number of peoples. It has observed that business organization and its management can approach the advertisement campaign through social media and Google search engine optimization process, which increases the reach of consumers far better in comparison to the other firms. On the other hand, the company introduces catalogs and different offers through journals and articles during different ovations and holidays to increase the attention of consumers in the marketplace. With the engagement of proper strategy implementation, the company can increase its business activates in the national and global wine industry significantly.</span><br></li>
</ul>
<div><br></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px">To achieve above average profits compare to other industry players in the long run, Sandlands Passalacqua needs to develop a sustainable competitive advantage. Industry analysis using Porter Five Forces can help Sandlands Passalacqua in casename case study to map the various forces and identify spaces where Sandlands Passalacqua can position itself.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"> </span><span style="font-size: 20px">By doing Industry analysis using Porter Five Forces, Sandlands Vineyards can develop four generic competitive strategies.</span></div>
<div><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>The four generic competitive strategies that can be pursued</h2></span></font><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>:</h2></span></font></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>Cost Leadership</h2></span><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>:</h2></span><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2><br></h2></span></font></div>
<ul>
<li><span style="font-size: 20px">In cost leadership, Sandlands Vineyards can set out to become the low cost producer in its industry. How it can become cost leader varies based on the industry forces and structure. In pursuing cost leadership strategy, Sandlands Passalacqua can assess Đ (pursuit of economies of scale, proprietary technology, supply chain management options, diversification of suppliers, preferential access to raw materials) and other factors. </span><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 23px"><br></span></font></li>
</ul>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><b><span style="font-size: 20px">Di</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 20px">ffe</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 20px">re</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 20px">nt</span></b><b><span style="font-size: 20px">iation: </span></b><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<ul>
<li><span style="font-size: 20px">Sandlands Passalacqua can also pursue differentiation strategy based on the industry forces description in casename case study. In a differentiation strategy Sandlands Passalacqua can seek to be unique in its industry by providing a value proposition that is cherished by buyers. Sandlands Passalacqua can select one or more attributes that Ęcan uniquely position it in the eyes of the customers for a specific needs. The goal is to seek premium price because of differentiation and uniqueness of the offering. Industry analysis using Porter Five Forces can help Sandlands Passalacqua to avoid spaces that are already over populated by the competitors.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></li>
</ul>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>The Focus Strategy has two variants</h2></span><span style="font-size: 23px"><h2>:</h2></span><font face=".AppleSystemUIFont"><span style="font-size: 20px"><h2><br></h2></span></font></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px">(a) In cost focus a Sandlands Vineyards can seek a cost advantage in its </span><span style="font-size: 20px">chosen</span><span style="font-size: 20px"> segment in case</span><span style="font-size: 20px"> </span><span style="font-size: 20px">category.(b) In Differentiation strategy Sandlands Vineyards can differentiate itself in a target segment in its industry.Both variants of the focus strategy rest on differences between a Sandlands Vineyards Ős target segment and other segments in the industry.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
<div><h2>PorterŐs 5 Forces, Competitive Forces &amp Industry Analysis</h2><h2>:</h2><h2><br></h2></div>
<div><br></div>
<div><span style="font-size: 19px">The core objective of strategists and leaders in an organization is to help the organization to build a sustainable competitive advantage and thwart competitive challenges.Step 1 Đ Defining relevant industry for Sandlands Passalacqua in casestudyStep 2 Đ Identify the competitors and group them based on the segments within the industryStep 3- Assess the Porter Five Forces in relation to the industry and assess which forces are strong and which forces are weak.Step 4 - Determine overall industry structure and test analysis of consistencyStep 5 Đ Analyze recent and future changes in each forcesStep 6 Đ Identify aspects of industry structure based on Porter 5 Forces that might be influenced by competitors and new entrants.</span><span style="font-size: 20px"><br></span></div>
